response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack software engineer with strong web/mobile/DevOps background, but the role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, performance architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. There is no evidence of GPU architecture work, SIMD/MIMD experience, deep learning workload optimization, or computer architecture credentials (no PhD/MS in relevant field, no chip-level work). The D-language low-latency and C/C++ experience are tangentially relevant but far below the bar for a Principal Performance Architect on AI accelerators.
strengths: Long tenure with low-latency systems (DLang async I/O, high-throughput microservices)
Some C/C++ experience at National Instruments on engineering tooling
Senior-level breadth across full stack and DevOps with strong testing discipline
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or chip architecture experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep learning workload optimization background
Educational path (BS only) without the 8+ years of *relevant* hardware/performance experience required

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DNN optimization experience
